meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: Overcoming the 'No Project Ideas' Block
content.seo_description: Learn how to use AI debugging to turn vague concepts into production-ready code. A high-level guide for students using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' idea. Use AI to debug existing open-source codebases or prototype incomplete features. By force-feeding your IDE broken, ambitious logic, you trigger AI to explain the architecture, effectively teaching you how to build complex SaaS features while resolving your creative paralysis through technical immersion.
content.intro:
The blank editor is a graveyard of ambition. You sit there, fingers hovering over the keys, paralyzed by the 'no project ideas' syndrome that plagues every aspiring developer. The gap between your current skill level and the vision in your head feels like a canyon, and without a clear roadmap, you simply stall.

But here is the secret: you don't need a groundbreaking idea to become a senior engineer. You need a feedback loop. By leveraging AI to debug and refactor complex, existing codebases, you bypass the 'blank page' problem entirely. We aren't just writing code; we are deconstructing how professional software is actually built, using AI as your mentor to turn frustration into a portfolio-ready project.

steps.title: Selecting a 'Broken' Starter Project
steps.description: Find an open-source project that is slightly beyond your current comfort level but has known issues or missing documentation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this repository structure. Identify the three most complex modules and explain how they communicate data between the API layer and the database.

steps.title: Injecting Intentional Faults
steps.description: Intentionally break a core function to force the AI to explain the underlying logic during the repair process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: I have removed the authentication middleware logic from this project. Walk me through the architectural requirements to rebuild it from scratch using JWT best practices.

steps.title: Refactoring via AI-Guided Debugging
steps.description: Use AI to identify performance bottlenecks in the codebase, turning a basic script into an optimized utility.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review this data processing loop. Identify why it is O(n^2) and suggest an O(n) implementation that utilizes a hash map.

steps.title: Building the Missing Feature
steps.description: Now that you understand the architecture, add a feature that was originally missing from the project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Based on the existing codebase, draft the implementation for a new 'Export to CSV' feature that integrates with the current service layer.

steps.title: Automating Test Coverage
steps.description: Ensure your new feature works by having the AI generate comprehensive unit tests for your changes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests for the new export feature, covering edge cases like empty datasets and connection timeouts.

pro_tips: Always ask the AI to explain the 'why' behind a fix, not just the 'how', to build your internal mental model.
Use Claude Code to generate documentation comments for every function you touch; it forces the AI to summarize complex logic.
When debugging, feed the AI the stack trace first, then the code snippet—never dump the whole file at once.
Treat every bug as a feature request; if you can't fix it, use the error message to pivot the project into a new tool.
Use Antigravity to map out the dependency graph of your project before you start changing lines of code.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully debugged and extended your first project, document your process in a README.md and deploy the application to Vercel or Railway to finalize your portfolio piece.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to turn any open-source codebase into a learning laboratory. Stop waiting for inspiration and start manipulating existing systems to build your own expertise. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
